children.name: Attagis
children.description: The larger of the two seedsnipe genera, with stout bodies, short legs, and strong bills adapted for a herbivorous diet. These are plump, grouse-like birds of the high Andes and southern Patagonia that feed almost entirely on plant material. Their cryptic plumage blends perfectly with the rocks and scree of their alpine habitat.

children.name: Attagis gayi
children.description: Found in the high Andes from Ecuador to southern Chile and Argentina, inhabiting barren rocky slopes and puna grasslands above the treeline. Its rufous belly and heavily streaked upperparts provide excellent camouflage among the rocks. It feeds on seeds, buds, and leaves, often seen in small coveys foraging together.

children.name: Attagis malouinus
children.description: Inhabits the southernmost tip of South America in Tierra del Fuego and the Falkland Islands, where it lives on windswept moorlands and coastal tussock. Its white belly and finely patterned grey-brown upperparts help it vanish among the lichen-covered rocks. It is remarkably cold-tolerant, remaining resident through harsh Patagonian winters.

children.name: Thinocorus
children.description: Smaller and more delicate than Attagis, with relatively longer legs and a more terrestrial, quail-like posture. These seedsnipes are among the few birds that can subsist entirely on a vegetable diet in the harsh conditions of the high Andes and Patagonian steppe.

children.name: Thinocorus rumicivorus
children.description: The smallest seedsnipe, found from the high Andes of Peru and Bolivia south to the lowland steppes of Patagonia. The male has a distinctive grey breast and face bordered by a dark line, while the female is more uniformly streaked. It is named for its diet of dock and other plant seeds.

children.name: Thinocorus orbignyianus
children.description: Ranges from the high Andes of Peru south to Tierra del Fuego, occupying rocky slopes and puna grasslands. The male has a pale grey breast with a distinctive rufous border separating it from the white belly. Its diet consists primarily of leaves, buds, and seeds of alpine plants.
